description: RGB shows coastal urban area with partial cloud and prominent brown sediment deposits covering roads and structures. SWIR reveals an extensive bright-blue inundation band along the entire coastline and into the city, confirming active widespread flooding.
labeler_notes: Partial cloud reduces coverage but flood signal is unambiguous in SWIR; sediment clearly visible in RGB along the wadi path.

description: RGB shows open ocean surface with scattered clouds and no discernible land. SWIR is predominantly dark water with no ground-level features visible.
labeler_notes: Tile falls over open sea; no terrain present to evaluate.

description: RGB is cloud-free and shows the coastal city with prominent brown sediment scar channels cutting through the urban grid, indicating recent flood scour. SWIR confirms urban fabric (bright yellow-white) with thin blue linear channels tracing the wadi path through the city — residual flood corridor still visible.
labeler_notes: Peak flood passed; post-event sediment scarring and channel erosion well documented in this clear acquisition ten days after peak.

description: RGB is almost entirely black with only faint teal gradients near the lower edge, consistent with a night-time or near-zero-reflectance acquisition. SWIR is uniformly black with no retrievable signal.
labeler_notes: Effectively a no-data tile; possibly night pass or severe underexposure.

description: RGB shows the coastal city clearly with some thin cloud and notably turbid (brown-green) nearshore water indicating sustained sediment discharge from flood-affected wadis. SWIR shows the urban area with green patches in surrounding fields and faint blue-toned linear channels through the city suggesting residual flood traces.
labeler_notes: One month post-peak; turbid plume still visible offshore and flood-scoured channels remain distinguishable — qualifies as HIGH aftermath.

description: RGB shows dark open ocean with scattered small cumulus clouds and no land visible. SWIR is nearly entirely black with only the bright cloud tops discernible.
labeler_notes: Ocean-only tile; no ground to assess.

description: RGB shows suburban and agricultural land on the left half with the right half obscured by cloud. SWIR confirms healthy bright-green vegetation on the visible left portion with no flood or fire signature; cloud dominates the right side.
labeler_notes: Pre-event baseline capture; cloud limits right-half interpretation but no hazard evidence in visible portion.

description: RGB is approximately 70% saturated white cloud with only the left strip of urban area visible. SWIR is similarly dominated by bright cloud returns with only a narrow left-edge strip showing green vegetation.
labeler_notes: Cloud cover is too extensive to draw any meaningful hazard conclusion; SKIP per policy.

description: RGB shows a mix of urban and agricultural terrain with pale, turbid water visible in the lower-right quadrant. SWIR reveals a large contiguous dark-blue region occupying roughly the right half of the frame, a definitive signal of widespread active inundation across agricultural and peri-urban land.
labeler_notes: Flood extent in SWIR is dramatic; the turbid RGB appearance corroborates fresh flood water with suspended sediment — high-priority downlink.

description: RGB shows an urban strip on the left with open, brown muddy terrain to the right and hints of standing water. SWIR displays an intensely dark-blue zone covering the majority of the right portion, confirming large-scale flood inundation abutting the urban edge.
labeler_notes: Flood water directly adjacent to urban area; clear threat to infrastructure visible in both channels.
